channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: An air traffic controller was juggling extra roles during the LaGuardia plane crash
item.description: The National Transportation Safety Board said it has concerns about air traffic controllers who work the midnight shift taking on extra work in an airspace as busy as LaGuardia's.
item.content:encoded:
The National Transportation Safety Board said it has concerns about air traffic controllers who work the midnight shift taking on extra work in an airspace as busy as LaGuardia's.

(Image credit: Yuki Iwamura)
item.dc:creator: Ayana Archie

item.title: New Mexico jury says Meta harms children's mental health and safety, violating state law
item.description: The jury agreed that Meta engaged in "unconscionable" trade practices that unfairly took advantage of the vulnerabilities of and inexperience of children. Jurors found there were thousands of violations, each counting separately toward a penalty of $375 million.
item.content:encoded:
The jury agreed that Meta engaged in "unconscionable" trade practices that unfairly took advantage of the vulnerabilities of and inexperience of children. Jurors found there were thousands of violations, each counting separately toward a penalty of $375 million.

(Image credit: Jim Weber/Santa Fe New Mexican)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: A former TSA security chief weighs in on the chaos at U.S. airports
item.description: TSA staffing shortages have led to hours-long lines for travelers at many airports. Keith Jeffries, the former TSA security director at Los Angeles International Airport shares insights on the crisis.
item.content:encoded: TSA staffing shortages have led to hours-long lines for travelers at many airports. Keith Jeffries, the former TSA security director at Los Angeles International Airport shares insights on the crisis.
item.dc:creator: Jordan-Marie Smith

item.title: Sailing season kicks off with a sock-burning party in Annapolis, Md.
item.description: In Annapolis, Md., people gather each year to usher in the warmer weather by making a sacrifice – of their socks. The springtime tradition is the unofficial start of the Chesapeake Bay sailing season.
item.content:encoded: In Annapolis, Md., people gather each year to usher in the warmer weather by making a sacrifice – of their socks. The springtime tradition is the unofficial start of the Chesapeake Bay sailing season.
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman

item.title: Senate confirms Trump's pick for new role of fraud enforcement at Justice Department
item.description: The confirmation comes just days after the White House announced details of its own task force to pursue fraud in government programs.
item.content:encoded:
The confirmation comes just days after the White House announced details of its own task force to pursue fraud in government programs.

(Image credit: Anna Moneymaker)
item.dc:creator: Jaclyn Diaz

item.title: A professional cornhole player and quadruple amputee is arrested for murder
item.description: Dayton Webber, 27, is accused of shooting a man in his car during an argument. He has shared his story of becoming a pro athlete after losing his arms and legs to a childhood bacterial infection.
item.content:encoded:
Dayton Webber, 27, is accused of shooting a man in his car during an argument. He has shared his story of becoming a pro athlete after losing his arms and legs to a childhood bacterial infection.

(Image credit: Kevin Sullivan)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: Airstrikes may have destroyed Iran's last F-14s, ending a long, strange saga
item.description: The F-14 was made famous in Top Gun. The U.S. sold the planes to Iran in the 1970s, only for the two countries to become enemies. Iran kept its F-14s flying for decades in the face of U.S. sanctions.
item.content:encoded:
The F-14 was made famous in Top Gun. The U.S. sold the planes to Iran in the 1970s, only for the two countries to become enemies. Iran kept its F-14s flying for decades in the face of U.S. sanctions.

(Image credit: U.S. Navy)
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman

item.title: ICE deployments created chaos for cities and cost them millions, NPR analysis finds
item.description: Local leaders report already-strapped police departments racked up overtime bills in the millions while others report a multi-million dollar hit to business during the worst ICE surges.
item.content:encoded: Local leaders report already-strapped police departments racked up overtime bills in the millions while others report a multi-million dollar hit to business during the worst ICE surges.
item.dc:creator: Jaclyn Diaz

item.title: As parents age, their children face hard choices about when to take the car keys
item.description: States have many policies to stop risky older drivers from renewing their licenses. But in practice, it's often adult children who must decide when to take the car keys away from an aging parent.
item.content:encoded:
States have many policies to stop risky older drivers from renewing their licenses. But in practice, it's often adult children who must decide when to take the car keys away from an aging parent.

(Image credit: Joel Rose)
item.dc:creator: Joel Rose

item.title: Relatives and advocates seek answers after Afghan asylum seeker dies in ICE custody
item.description: Family members and U.S. veterans' advocates await answers about the death in ICE custody of an Afghan asylum seeker who aided U.S. Army Special Forces during the war in Afghanistan.
item.content:encoded: Family members and U.S. veterans' advocates await answers about the death in ICE custody of an Afghan asylum seeker who aided U.S. Army Special Forces during the war in Afghanistan.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Schneider
